texto: Persistent problems, however unpleasant they may seem, contain the unprocessed and unexamined thoughts and feelings that, if left alone, keep you from your greatness. That’s why the pain, emptiness, and longing you feel can be your greatest gift—it can motivate you to examine parts of yourself that have been overlooked, forgotten, or hidden. It’s the irritant of sand in the oyster, which is the impetus for the pearl. In walking the conscious life path, you reveal your deepest Reality, layer by layer. You come home.
explicacao: Citação de Jennifer Howard sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Persistent problems, however unpleasant they may seem, contain the unprocessed and unexamined tho…” articula motivacao, inspirational-attitude, psychology em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e estudos.

texto: What? A man who could admit to his feelings? They really do exist!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jennifer Howard que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “What? A man who could admit to his feelings? They really do exist!”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, feelings, man, com foco em man e the-healing-heart.

texto: How do you know if your inner voice or sense of guidance is your deeper-self speaking its wisdom, your unconscious childhood programming, or the voice of your Aunt Matilda? Discerning the difference between an automatic response stemming from family beliefs, childhood distortions, or culture, and guidance from our connection with our higher/deeper self, makes life, success, and relationships so much easier.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jennifer Howard que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “How do you know if your inner voice or sense of guidance is your deeper-self speaking its wisdom,…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, psychology, psychology-spirituality, com foco em psychology-spirituality.

texto: For most of us enlightenment is not a destination or graduation into a permanent higher state of consciousness, but a moment-by-moment experience constantly fluctuating between degrees of wholeness and limited consciousness. I like to call this “enlightening-ment.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jennifer Howard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “For most of us enlightenment is not a destination or graduation into a permanent higher state of …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, conscious-living, enlightenment pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de formatura e enlightenment.
